headline: Master Data Analysis, Master Your Workflow, Master Your Projects

stories.title: Data Analysis is the Skill to Learn in 2026
stories.summary: <cite index="2-5,2-6,2-7">In today's data-driven world, consultants need strong data analysis skills since businesses rely on data for decisions and consultants are expected to turn complex data into actionable insights using tools like Excel, Power BI, and Tableau.</cite> Lowkey, if you're not picking up SQL, Python, or advanced data literacy, you're already falling behind.
stories.takeaway: Data fluency isn't optional anymore—it's literally your competitive edge fr

stories.title: Quick Excel Trick: Use =TODAY() for Auto-Updating Dates
stories.summary: <cite index="20-9">To enter a date that will update to the current date each time you reopen a worksheet or recalculate a formula, type =TODAY() in an empty cell, and then press ENTER.</cite> Saves you from manually updating those deadlines every single day tbh.
stories.takeaway: One formula, no more manual date updates—game changer for project tracking

stories.title: Rocketlane Nitro: AI-Powered Execution Tool for Consultants
stories.summary: <cite index="42-4,42-5,42-7">Rocketlane launched Nitro, an AI-powered execution platform designed to automate delivery across professional services projects—the first agentic execution platform that embeds AI agents directly into project workflows to automate elements like migrations, system configuration, documentation and testing.</cite> Lowkey changes how consulting firms scale without hiring more people.
stories.takeaway: AI handles the repetitive stuff, your team focuses on strategy & client relationships

watchNext.title: AI Tools Every Consultant Should Know About in 2026
watchNext.why: Covers Think Cell, Olive, Forecast, and ChatGPT—all saving consulting teams serious time on chart creation, vendor eval, and resource forecasting

watchNext.title: Working with AI as a Data Professional in 2026
watchNext.why: Real talk on how to stay relevant when AI is literally doing half your job—focus on storytelling and human judgment, not competing with bots
